run_label: late-morning run (third of day) — operator DIRECTIVE-003 pickup, PROP-009r revision
mode_reason: Third operator directive of the day — DIRECTIVE-20260418-003-prop-009-revision.json (49588 bytes, 4 minutes before this run) arrived requesting a revision of PROP-009 driven by a bundled hostile-review agent with 3 critical + 4 major + 3 moderate + 3 minor findings. Directive is explicit about format constraints (raw-text line arrays, dry_run_check preflights, flag-file instead of env-var), requires a fresh retroactive audit (not copied from PROP-009), and requires a `disagreements_with_review` section with explicit pushback on any fix tinker believes is wrong. This is a high-priority PROP deliverable. Mode 2 infrastructure findings (FUSE staleness unchanged from prior run, disk 83% flat) are still present but not newly urgent. Mode 3 cost-engineering deferred (previous Mode 3 ran 2h ago at 09:50Z). Mode 1 pipeline health is green-but-busy (queue grew to depth 4 as operator re-pushed the 3 unreviewed items and added EXP-184 with `require_matching_review_file:true` as a stopgap ahead of PROP-009r enforcement).

findings.description: PROP-009r written per DIRECTIVE-20260418-003 — revises PROP-009 to address 3 critical + 4 major + 3 moderate findings from the bundled hostile review. Key additions vs PROP-009: (a) raw-text line arrays instead of escaped find/replace strings, (b) dry_run_check preflights per block, (c) flag-file enforcement toggle (clone-side `monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag`) instead of env-var (shell expands `${VAR:-default}` before node sees it), (d) popped_by_queue_id consumption tracking (prevents a single curmudgeon review from being claimed by two pushes of the same target_id), (e) unionReviewFiles() dual-read across WORKSPACE (FUSE) + CLONE, (f) narrow 3-field operator bypass instead of free-form JSON, (g) fresh retroactive audit (not copied from PROP-009) showing 23/50 unmatched pops in the 50-entry history window (matches concrete-fixes-agent count exactly), (h) disagreements_with_review section with explicit pushback where I disagreed with the review (see PROP-009r.disagreements_with_review).
findings.evidence: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-009r.json (65732 bytes, 9 files_to_modify + 1 files_to_create). Verification pattern: `[ -n "${CLEAN_CLONE}" ] && grep -q 'PROP-009 precondition' monitor/prompts/decider.md && grep -q 'PROP-009 precondition' monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md && grep -q 'prop-009-enforce.flag' monitor/prompts/decider.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN`.
findings.why_not_self_fixed: Coordinated multi-file prompt change needs operator approval. PROP-009r is now the authoritative proposal on file.

findings.description: Self-caught apply bug in the first PROP-009r draft: 3 of 9 find/replace blocks had anchors that did not match main HEAD. Block #2 (curmudgeon.md Step 0b line 7) targeted the FUSE workspace's shorter version of the line; main HEAD carries a longer 'escape hatch + git-owned' variant. Block #7 (DATA-SCHEMAS.md) anchored on a non-existent `## priority-queue.json` heading. Block #8 (BUILD-AND-CHANGE.md) anchored on `### File Ownership Rules (Phase 1)` which actually lives in CLAUDE.md, not BUILD-AND-CHANGE.md. All three were caught and fixed in-place before the report was written (re-verification: 9/9 MATCH against main HEAD). The bug is a direct consequence of the FUSE architectural-gap finding in infrastructure_findings — the workspace served stale prompt content that the initial draft matched against.
findings.evidence: Pre-fix check: `node -e "...p.proposed_fix.files_to_modify.forEach(f=>body.includes(f.find_lines.join('\n')))..."` returned MATCH for 6/9, MISS for 3/9. Post-fix (scratch/fix_prop_009r.js): 9/9 MATCH. PROP-009r.disagreements_with_review.apply_audit_fixes_2026_04_18_T11_50 documents the three fixes and the lesson.
findings.why_fixed: Apply-audit is exactly the class of bug the operator's hostile review C1 finding targeted (though C1 was wrong about the cause: PROP-009's substrings actually did match verbatim). The hostile review's insistence on raw-line arrays and dry_run_check preflights gave us the machinery to both detect and repair this class of bug. Documented the root cause in PROP-009r and the lesson-for-future-tinker note.

findings.description: Directly linked to the FUSE architectural gap: this tinker run's own work was corrupted (and then self-repaired) by reading stale prompt content from the workspace. The apply-audit catch worked, but two observations: (a) the repair required a fresh raw.githubusercontent.com fetch for cross-check, so tinker is now implicitly dependent on the public raw URL being reachable (it is, but that's an additional failure surface), and (b) if the 2-line diff had happened to fall INSIDE the Step 0b line 7 find block rather than 2 lines away from it, the bug would have silently miscompiled the fix. PROP-010 (reverse sync) would eliminate this class of risk entirely.
findings.evidence: scratch/fix_prop_009r.js re-verification output: 9/9 MATCH after re-anchoring against main HEAD.

self_fixes_applied.risk_if_reverted: Operator would hit dry_run_check failures on 3 blocks when applying PROP-009r, requiring a round-trip with tinker.

deliverables_addressed.critical_c1_raw_text_lines: yes — find_lines/replace_lines are JSON string arrays
deliverables_addressed.critical_c2_dry_run_preflights: yes — every block has a dry_run_check with grep -F and expect_exit
deliverables_addressed.major_m1_narrow_operator_bypass: yes — 3 explicit fields (pushed_by contains 'operator' + require_matching_review_file:false + operator_bypass_reason required)

deliverables_addressed.major_m3_dual_read_workspace_and_clone: yes — unionReviewFiles() reads from both FUSE WORKSPACE and CLONE

disagreements_with_review: C1 partial: the hostile review claimed PROP-009's escaped find/replace strings would fail to apply verbatim. This is WRONG — `content.includes(PROP-009.find_string)` returned TRUE for both decider.md and curmudgeon.md against the FUSE workspace. However, tinker adopted the array-of-raw-lines format anyway because it's unambiguous for any applier (including hand-applied sed/patch) and survives future editor reformats better than escape-encoded strings.
M1 accepted as specified: narrow 3-field operator bypass is correct. The review's proposed exact fields are what PROP-009r implements.

Mod2 7-day escalation removed from PROP-009r: the review proposed an automated 7-day-stale shadow escalation; tinker judged this non-load-bearing and removed it from the mandated migration_plan. If a shadow period stretches past 7 days with no operator flip, tinker will flag that in the next summary but will not auto-escalate. Operator can countervote.
new_proposals.fresh_retroactive_audit_result: 23/50 unmatched pops in the 50-entry history window (per_day_unmatched: 2026-04-18 = 18, 2026-04-17 = 5, 2026-04-16 = 0; per_target_type_unmatched: win-detail-rewrite 12, section-rewrite 8, expansion-draft 3). Matches the concrete-fixes-agent count exactly — no classification errors. Current corpus queue_id coverage: 56/304 review files (18.4%) — this is why the soft `reviewed_at > pushed_at` fallback is load-bearing during migration. Once corpus crosses ~80% queue_id coverage AND at least one `strict_queue_id` pop lands in history, the enforce flag is safe to flip.

PROP-009r adds a single additive-edit exception for popped_by_queue_id on curmudgeon reviews; this exception is the ONLY new wrinkle in the ownership rules and is explicitly carved out in PROP-009r's files_to_modify #8. If PROP-009r lands, the ownership table in CLAUDE.md grows by ~3 lines; no structural change.

recommendations.description: Review and approve PROP-009r. Single-commit application recommended (all 9 file edits land together). Shadow migration controlled by presence/absence of `monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag` on the decider's clone; start with flag absent (shadow), flip after audit_criteria_before_flipping_enforce is satisfied. Rollback = revert commit OR delete flag file.

Decide whether to formally close PROP-009 (old) in the tracker, superseded by PROP-009r. Recommend marking PROP-009.status = 'superseded' with a pointer to PROP-009r to avoid confusion if someone greps for 'PROP-009' in the future.

Next Mode 2 run: write PROP-010 for git→workspace reverse sync in workspace-sync agent. Today's apply-audit catch is concrete evidence this class of bug can and does occur; fixing the source (FUSE staleness) is cheaper than repeated cross-check-against-main mitigation.
PROP-005 merge-or-close decision. Proposed for 13+ consecutive runs, no forward momentum. If the pipeline stays in BAU, close as wontfix; if it returns to quiet mode in the next 7 days, merge.

Consider promoting the 'cross-check find/replace anchors against main HEAD raw URL' pattern from this run's apply-audit catch into tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md as a MANDATORY step when drafting a PROP that edits git-owned prompt files. Low cost, high return: it catches stale-FUSE-induced anchor mismatches before the operator does.

the_other_side.prop_009r_strongest_objection: PROP-009r's weight (9 files_to_modify, 7-step apply order, 2 new fields in history, 1 new ownership exception) is significant for what started as a substring-match bug fix. A skeptic could argue the hostile review over-engineered the fix: the original PROP-009's find/replace strings DID match the files verbatim (tinker verified this before writing PROP-009r), so C1 was factually wrong; C3's flag-file advantage over env-var is real but the env-var bug it prevents was hypothetical; M2's consumption tracking is correct but it only matters when an operator re-pushes the same target_id twice in quick succession (observable in history only 2 times in 50 entries). Counter: the bug we're fixing destroyed adversarial review for 10 consecutive queue items over 72h, and that's EXACTLY the class of bug that prior-assumption-checking catches poorly. PROP-009r's extra machinery (dry_run_check, flag file, popped_by_queue_id) adds ~30 lines of prompt code and ~5min of operator apply time — cheap insurance against the same class of silent-failure bug recurring. Additionally, the apply-audit catch in this very run VALIDATED one of the hostile review's concerns (raw-line arrays + dry_run_check) even though C1 itself was wrong about the immediate cause.
the_other_side.directive_003_rate_objection: Three operator directives in one day (001, 002, 003 at 07:17, 10:00, 12:25 UTC) suggests either (a) the operator is working through a backlog of accumulated pipeline concerns, or (b) the pipeline is generating more fires than tinker's 1-per-day cadence can handle. Counter: the three directives have different root causes (change-detection cooldown; post-integration review gap; PROP-009 hostile review follow-up). They are not a pattern of one underlying issue, they're three different real issues surfaced by independent operator inspections. Tinker's cadence is not a bottleneck; the correct response is to handle directives as they arrive, which today's runs did (09:50Z Mode 3 → 10:13Z Mode 4 → 11:50Z Mode 4). If tomorrow brings another 3-directive day, revisit.
the_other_side.apply_audit_self_catch_cost_objection: The apply-audit that caught 3 mis-anchored blocks is a form of 'inspect ones own work' that feels suspiciously like rework. A skeptic could argue tinker should have drafted the find/replace blocks against main HEAD from the start, not the FUSE workspace, and saved the repair step entirely. Counter: the PROP-009r first draft was built from content already loaded into tinker's context (FUSE workspace files that prior steps had read). Re-fetching every anchor against main HEAD at draft time would have added ~6 curls and ~30s runtime. The inspect-then-repair pattern is cheaper in expectation (most find blocks match fine) and provides a natural checkpoint for updating find_lines when the draft is otherwise complete. The correct process fix is NOT 'always fetch main HEAD before drafting' but 'always run dry_run_check (or equivalent) after drafting' — which is exactly what PROP-009r's own dry_run_check preflights mandate for the operator during apply. So tinker is now eating its own dogfood.
the_other_side.fuse_architectural_objection: The FUSE staleness finding has been moderate-severity for 3 consecutive days without being upgraded to major. A skeptic could argue this is classification drift — if a moderate finding persists unfixed long enough, either (a) it's actually minor and should be downgraded, or (b) the severity ladder is wrong. Counter: the severity is calibrated correctly. Moderate means 'degrades quality but protected by redundancy; no hard failures yet'. Today's apply-audit catch is a hard-failure candidate that was caught by redundancy (cross-check against main HEAD); that's exactly the moderate classification in action. The day redundancy fails and a stale-read lands in committed output unchecked, it upgrades to major. PROP-010 removes the redundancy dependency entirely.
the_other_side.shadow_migration_objection: PROP-009r proposes the operator flip `prop-009-enforce.flag` only after 'at least one strict_queue_id pop in history AND corpus queue_id coverage stabilizes at ≥80%'. The 80% threshold is arbitrary — why not 70%, 90%, or 100%? Counter: 80% is pragmatic (most historical reviews lack queue_id because the field was added today; full backfill is manual and expensive). The fallback path (soft reviewed_at > pushed_at) handles the remaining 20% cleanly. 70% would risk too many soft-fallback pops masking a strict-match bug; 90% delays enforcement unnecessarily. If the operator wants a different threshold, they can edit it before approving — it lives in PROP-009r.migration_plan.audit_criteria_before_flipping_enforce as a single JSON field.

notes_to_future_tinker: PROP-009r is 65732 bytes. All 9 find_lines blocks re-verified against main HEAD (not FUSE workspace) at 2026-04-18T11:50Z. If operator applies PROP-009r and hits a dry_run_check failure, cross-check whether main HEAD drifted between 11:50Z and apply time — if so, update the affected block and re-verify.

The apply-audit self-catch pattern (load find_lines against main HEAD raw, verify content.includes()) should be promoted to a mandatory step in tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md for any PROP that edits prompt files. Today was the second time this run found itself fixing its own work; formalize the pattern.
PROP-010 (git→workspace reverse sync) is now more urgent after today's apply-audit catch. Today's bug was caught; a future one may not be. Schedule a dedicated Mode 2 run for PROP-010.

Curmudgeon's next run at ~11:20Z will consume the queue. qid 175 EXP-183 (no flag) should get a normal review; qid 176/177/178 (with require_matching_review_file) should also get normal reviews — the flag only matters at POP time (decider side), not at review time (curmudgeon side). Verify next run that curmudgeon wrote queue_id fields on its reviews; if not, that's a separate issue requiring curmudgeon to be updated.
PROP-009.json (old) still has status='proposed' in the tracker. Decide whether to flip to 'superseded' explicitly or rely on the supersedes field in PROP-009r.
Third tinker run of the day — if a 4th directive arrives, load the dispatcher + proposals module only (skip Mode 2/Mode 3 reference files). Mode 4 context cost is manageable.
